2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.

36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: byte-parity corpus must pass.
43	4. Integration: dirty room may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
44	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced

1	# REPORT4.md — full route surface (the dispatcher owns the whole request path)
2	
3	Round 4 of the `weblayer` clean-room cluster (Unit A, web dispatch). Rounds 1-3
4	reproduced the response bodies and the interactive read/proof/edit state machine.
5	This round extends `route::Route`/`route::Toplevel` and `Server<P: ProverOps>` to
6	the REST of the observed surface so the dispatcher owns the consumer's entire
7	request path.
8	
9	Black-box only: live probing of the sanctioned oracle ([R40]-[R4B], ports
10	3140-3143, Tutorial/NSLPK3/KCL diff theory) plus a corpus key census. No file
11	under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ was read; the only tamarin-rs touch was executing
12	the sanctioned binary (`interactive`, `--diff`, `--port=`).
13	
14	## Surface added
15	
16	* Top level (`route::Toplevel`): `GET /` (index) + `POST /` (upload),
17	  `/robots.txt`, `/favicon.ico` (-> static icon), `/kill` (cancel a search),
18	  `/static/**` (filesystem assets), plus `/thy/...`.
19	* Theory scope: `reload`, `download/{file}`, `get_and_append/{file}`, and the diff
20	  (`equiv`) analogues `diffProof`/`diffMethod`/`diffrules`/`autoproveDiff`/
21	  `autoproveAll`.
22	* Error family: the 404/400/405 Yesod default-layout pages, generalised from the
23	  round-1 single 404 template.
24	
25	## Behaviour pinned down (BEHAVIOR.md 14)
26	
27	* One global version-index namespace. Proof ops AND uploads each allocate a fresh
28	  index off the same monotonic counter; structural edits and `reload` mutate in
29	  place (counter untouched, versions retained). Reload does not reset the counter
30	  or drop modified versions ([R43],[R45]).
31	* Envelopes. Third JSON shape `{"alert":...}` (get_and_append; and a FAILED
32	  method/diffMethod, which does NOT bump the version). `download` = the `source`
33	  bytes with content type application/octet-stream, no Content-Disposition.
34	  `reload` = a JSON {redirect} (not a 303).
35	* Redirect caching. All 303s (edit/add/delete success, delete-not-found, favicon)
36	  carry Cache-Control: no-cache, must-revalidate + a fixed past Expires
37	  (Response.no_cache).
38	* Static. Content type by extension without charset; a miss is plain-text
39	  `File not found`.
40	* Delete branch (refines 13.3): found -> 303 overview/help; not found -> 303
41	  overview/delete/{name}. delete_lemma now returns Option.
42	* Diff mode (`--diff`, kind `equiv`): overview shell = trace shell with a
43	  `DiffTheory:` title, /thy/equiv/ links, and no "Append modified lemmas" item;
44	  diffMethod redirects to overview/diffProof/...
45	
46	## Two documented negative results
47	
48	* del/path and verify return 404 (not 405) for every method/shape in both modes
49	  and appear in ZERO manifest keys -- neither in the corpus nor live-observable in
50	  1.13.0. Routed to Handler::Other (-> 404); no bytes fabricated. ([R47])
51	* autoproveDiff/autoproveAll block the prover on the probe theory (curl timeouts),
52	  so their exact redirect bytes are unobservable here. Dispatched as proof ops
53	  (new version) with the redirect modelled by analogy to diffMethod/autoprove.
54	  ([R49])
55	
56	## Deliverables
57	
58	* src/route.rs -- `Toplevel` entry point; `Handler` gains Reload/Download/
59	  GetAndAppend/Edit/AutoproveDiff/AutoproveAll; `Main` gains DiffProof/DiffMethod/
60	  DiffRules; AutoproveDiff/AutoproveAll parsers; OverviewView gains DiffProof/Delete.
61	* src/dispatch.rs -- Server::dispatch now takes the whole Toplevel surface;
62	  Response gains no_cache; Request gains query; ProverOps grown minimally (pure
63	  data producers + Option-returning apply_method/delete_lemma).
64	* src/page.rs + src/shell_template.rs -- shell parameterised over ShellKind
65	  (trace/equiv); index page (render_root/render_root_row) + root/simple-page templates.
66	* src/assets.rs (new) -- static content-type map + fixed text bodies.
67	* src/errors.rs -- 404/400/405 from a shared simple-page template
68	  (notfound_template.rs removed).
69	* src/envelope.rs -- the {alert} shape.
70	* tests/dispatch4.rs (17 tests) + tests/fixtures/r4_* -- capture-backed
71	  byte-parity/behaviour tests; BEHAVIOR.md 14, QUERIES.log [R40]-[R4B].
72	
73	`cargo test` -> 75 passing (24 unit + 15 dispatch + 17 dispatch4 + 19 parity),
74	`cargo clippy --tests --examples` clean. Byte-parity vs live captures for
75	robots.txt, the Invalid-Arguments (400) and Method-Not-Supported (405) pages,
76	`Canceled request!`, download==source, reload redirect, the index page
77	(decomposition), and the equiv overview shell (decomposition); all re-verified
78	byte-identical on a fresh server instance ([R4B]). Round-1..3 tests unchanged in
79	intent (round-3 FakeProver updated for the Option-typed callbacks; assertions hold).
80	All servers stopped.
81	
82	## Honesty / scope notes
83	
84	* ProverOps returns opaque fragments; tests feed canned fragments so the asserted
85	  bytes are the WEB-LAYER assembly (envelope/status/Location/version/content-type/
86	  cache), never re-derived prover logic.
87	* Index-page rows (load time, origin) and upload/diff proof-state content are
88	  non-deterministic/prover fragments; the root page is byte-tested by decomposition

92	  passthrough is an integration-adapter concern (documented in dispatch.rs).

1 # BEHAVIOR.md — observed spec of the tamarin-prover interactive web UI 2 3 Derived entirely from black-box observation: 81 crawl manifests in 4 `oracle/captured_responses/` (captured OUTPUT) plus live probing of the 5 sanctioned server (`QUERIES.log` [L0]–[L4]). No prover source was read. 6 7 Terms: **web layer** = the code that dispatches routes, escapes, and wraps 8 strings into envelopes/pages. **prover fragment** = a string the prover produced 9 (pretty-printed terms, constraint systems, method names, DOT bodies) that the 10 web layer embeds opaquely. This spec pins down the web layer; prover fragments 11 are treated as inputs. 12 13 --- 14 15 ## 1. Route grammar 16 17 Every dynamic route has the shape: 18 19 ``` 20 /thy//// 21 ``` 22 23 * `` — analysis kind. Only `trace` observed (a `diff` kind is 24 plausible but never appears in the corpus). 25 * `` — theory **version** selector. In real requests it is a decimal 26 number. The manifests store it as the literal token `#`; live probing shows 27 `%23` returns **404**, so `#` is the crawl tool's placeholder meaning "the 28 server's current version", and every URL the server *emits* uses the resolved 29 numeric index. Applying a proof method creates a new version, so the index 30 seen in a response's links is generally larger than the one requested. 31 * `` — selects the response family: 32 33 | handler | body kind | notes | 34 |-----------------------------|-----------|-------| 35 | `main/…` | JSON | AJAX content pane; envelope `{html,title}` or `{redirect}` | 36 | `overview/…` | HTML | full theory-view page (see §5) | 37 | `intdot/…` | HTML | graph mini-page embedding `` (§8) | 38 | `interactive-graph-def/…` | DOT | Graphviz source for a proof node (§8) | 39 | `next/…`, `prev/…` | text | a single navigation URL (§9) | 40 | `autoprove/…` | JSON | `{redirect}`; **text** with HTTP status 0 on timeout | 41 | `source`, `message` | text | the theory source, verbatim (§9) | 42 43 `main/*` sub-handlers (observed, exhaustive over the corpus): 44 45 | sub-route | meaning | 46 |-----------|---------| 47 | `main/help` | theory header + wellformedness report + keyboard help | 48 | `main/message` | signature + construction/deconstruction rules | 49 | `main/rules` | multiset rewriting rules (+ restrictions) | 50 | `main/tactic` | tactic(s) listing | 51 | `main/cases/{raw\|refined}/{level}/{n}` | source cases | 52 | `main/lemma/{name}` | lemma summary | 53 | `main/proof/{lemma}/{path…}` | constraint system + applicable methods at a proof node; `path` is a `/`-joined sequence of case-name segments, root `_` | 54 | `main/method/{lemma}/{n}` | apply method `n` → `{redirect}` | 55 | `main/add/{pos}` | add-lemma form (`pos` = a lemma name or `` ⇒ `%3Cfirst%3E`) | 56 | `main/edit/{name}` | edit-lemma form | 57 | `main/delete/{name}` | delete-lemma confirmation | 58 59 `overview/*` mirrors `main/*` for the pages that render a full shell: 60 `overview/help`, `overview/proof/{lemma}/{path…}`. `intdot/*` and 61 `interactive-graph-def/*` take the same `proof/{lemma}/{path…}` (and 62 `cases/…`) tails. 63 64 ### Authoritative handler/kind census (all 81 manifests, 48824 dynamic routes) 65 66 Enumerated with a template pass ([Q020]–[Q021]); the response `kind` is fixed 67 per handler: 68 69 | handler | kind | count | argument shape | 70 |--------------------------|-------------|-------|----------------| 71 | `main` | json | 17609 | see `main/*` table above | 72 | `interactive-graph-def` | dot | 14705 | `proof/{lemma}[/{path…}]` | 73 | `intdot` | html | 14705 | `proof/{lemma}[/{path…}]` | 74 | `overview` | html | 473 | `help` \| `proof/{lemma}[/{path…}]` | 75 | `next` | text | 392 | `normal/proof/{lemma}` | 76 | `prev` | text | 392 | `normal/proof/{lemma}` | 77 | `autoprove` | json (text on timeout) | 392 | `idfs/{bound}/False/proof/{lemma}` | 78 | `source` | text | 81 | (none) | 79 | `message` | text | 81 | (none) | 80 81 `autoprove/{strategy}/{bound}/{allSol}/proof/{lemma}[/path…]` is the full 82 autoprove shape (see §13.2). #!/usr/bin/env bash
# Live reference-server wrapper for UNIT A round 5 probing (del/path + verify).
# Usage: wl_server5.sh start <file.spthy> <port> [extra tamarin flags...]
#        wl_server5.sh stop <port>
# Uses the --port=<n> (equals) form to avoid the workdir-positional bug.
# Live-server ports are constrained to 3100-3199 for this unit.
export PATH="/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin:$PATH"
BIN="/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover-testing/.stack-work/install/x86_64-linux-tinfo6/ec0cb11b1bfcf8776d45e0357bbc6d6ff2077f9222735af22115429c8cdfcef1/9.6.7/bin/tamarin-prover"
cmd="$1"; shift
case "$cmd" in
  start)
    f="$1"; port="$2"; shift 2
    d=$(mktemp -d)
    cp "$f" "$d/"
    nohup "$BIN" interactive "$d" --port="$port" "$@" \
      >"/tmp/wl5_$port.log" 2>&1 &
    echo $! > "/tmp/wl5_$port.pid"
    sleep 6
    echo "up on $port (pid $(cat /tmp/wl5_$port.pid)) dir $d"
    ;;
  stop)
    port="$1"
    kill "$(cat /tmp/wl5_$port.pid)" 2>/dev/null
    rm -f "/tmp/wl5_$port.pid"
    echo "stopped $port"
    ;;
esac

## 15. `del/path` and `verify` theory-path routes (Round 5)

Derived from live probing of the sanctioned oracle ([R50]–[R57], ports 3100–3105,
`RevealingSignatures`/issue193.spthy `debug` lemma for trace, KCL07-UK1 diff theory
for equiv) plus the four staged captures in `round5/` (`del_path.json`,
`del_path_bad.json`, `verify.json`, `verify_proof.json`). Reproduced in
`src/route.rs` (`Handler::DelPath` / `Handler::Verify` + the mode-aware
`ThyPath` grammar) and `src/dispatch.rs` (`Server::del_path` / `Server::verify`)
over three new `ProverOps` callbacks. All bodies live-verified byte-for-byte
against the oracle on fresh servers.

### 15.1 Shape, registration, method — reconciles §14.8
Both routes take a two-segment-plus **theory sub-path**:
`GET /thy/<kind>/<idx>/del/path/<theory-path>` and
`GET /thy/<kind>/<idx>/verify/<theory-path>` (the `path` literal is fixed for
`del`). Both are **GET-only**. Route matching is by a `PathMultiPiece` parse of the
theory-path, and method dispatch happens **after** that parse ([R55]):

| tail parse | method | result |
|------------|--------|--------|
| parses (a theory path) | GET | the route's logic (§15.3/§15.4) |
| parses | non-GET | `405` Method-Not-Supported (same page as §14.1) |
| does not parse | any | `404` full-HTML page echoing the full request path |

`del` without the `path` literal (`del/lemma/…`) and `del/path` with no further
tail → `404`.

### 15.2 The theory-path grammar is MODE-DEPENDENT ([R56])
The accepted heads differ by theory kind (tamarin's `TheoryPath` vs
`DiffTheoryPath` — `src/route.rs::ThyPath::parse(segs, diff)`):

* **trace**: `help` · `message` · `rules` · `tactic` ·
  `cases/{raw|refined}/{level}/{n}` · `lemma/{name}` · `proof/{lemma}[/seg…]` ·
  `method/{lemma}/{n}[/seg…]` · `add/{pos}` · `edit/{name}` · `delete/{name}`.
  `sources`, bare `cases`, `diffProof`, `diffrules`, and unknown heads → `404`.
* **equiv**: `help` · `diffrules` · `diffProof/{lemma}[/side…]` ·
  `diffMethod/{lemma}/{n}[/…]`. The trace heads (`proof`/`rules`/`message`/
  `tactic`/`cases`/`lemma`/`add`/`edit`/`delete`/`method`) → `404`.

### 15.3 `verify` — trace-only, never mutates ([R50],[R51],[R57])
`verify` is registered **only for trace theories** (in equiv every `verify/<x>`,
including `diffProof`/`help`, is `404` for GET *and* POST — the route is absent).
It **never** allocates a version or mutates the theory. For a parseable trace
theory-path:

* `proof/{lemma}[/path]` where **the lemma is present** → `200` JSON
  `{"redirect":"/thy/trace/<idx>/overview/proof/{lemma}[/path]"}` at the **same**
  index. The redirect target is `overview/` + the **verbatim** input path; the
  predicate is **lemma existence** (a bogus sub-node of a real lemma still
  redirects; a proof path to an absent lemma does not).
* every other path — non-proof heads, and `proof/{absent-lemma}` — → `200` JSON
  `{"html","title"}` = the theory **help pane**, byte-identical to `main/help`
  (title `Theory: <NAME>`) for all of them (7/7 `cmp`).

### 15.4 `del/path` — a proof operation (new version); path-typed alerts ([R52]–[R54],[R57])
`del/path` is registered in **both** kinds. For a parseable theory-path it either
deletes (a lemma or proof node → a **fresh version**, base retained) or answers a
JSON `{"alert"}`. The alert **string is selected by the path TYPE** (web-layer),
while success-vs-failure is a prover decision (an `Option`):

| path type | deletable → | not deletable → alert |
|-----------|-------------|-----------------------|
| `lemma/{name}` | `{"redirect":".../overview/lemma/{name}"}`, new version | `{"alert":"Sorry, but removing the selected lemma failed!"}` |
| `proof/{lemma}[/p]` (trace) / `diffProof/{lemma}[/p]` (equiv) | `{"redirect":".../overview/<proof\|diffProof>/{lemma}[/p]"}`, new version | `{"alert":"Sorry, but removing the selected proof step failed!"}` |
| any other head (help/message/rules/tactic/cases/method/add/edit/delete; equiv help/diffrules/diffMethod) | — (never deletable) | `{"alert":"Can't delete the given theory path!"}` (no prover call, no bump) |

`lemma/{absent}` → the lemma alert; `proof/{absent-lemma}` or a bogus sub-node →
the proof-step alert. The redirect target is `overview/` + the **verbatim** input
path at the freshly allocated index. Alerts allocate no version and mutate
nothing.

**Version-model reconciliation (with §13.1 / §14.3).** A deletable `del/path`
allocates a **new index off the same global monotonic counter** as
`method`/`autoprove` and leaves the base resolvable — it is a **proof operation**,
NOT an in-place structural edit (POST `edit/delete/{name}`, which stays at the same
index). Proven: `autoprove debug` → v2 (SOLVED); `del/path/proof/debug` on v2 → v3
(reset to `by sorry`, the deletion persisting in the new version) while v2 stays
SOLVED ([R53]).

### 15.5 ProverOps growth (Round 5)
Three additions (`src/dispatch.rs`): `lemma_present` (drives verify's
redirect-vs-help choice), `del_lemma_path` (`del/path/lemma`, `Option`), and
`del_proof_step` (`del/path/proof`|`diffProof`, `Option`, with a `diff` flag). All
transport — envelope shape, the three alert strings, redirect assembly, version
allocation, content type, and the `404`/`405` decisions — stays in `Server`.

- **verify** — trace-only (absent for any method in equiv), never mutates or bumps a version. `proof/<present-lemma>[/path]` → `{redirect}` to `overview/proof/…` (verbatim path, same index; predicate = lemma existence, a bogus sub-node still redirects); every other path (incl. `proof/<absent-lemma>`) → the `{html,title}` help pane, byte-identical to `main/help`.
- **del/path** — a **proof operation**: a deletable lemma/proof node allocates a **fresh version** off the same global monotonic counter as method/autoprove (base retained; deletion persists in the new version), redirecting to `overview/` + the verbatim path. Non-deletable paths return a JSON `{alert}` whose string is selected by **path type**: `"Sorry, but removing the selected lemma failed!"` / `"Sorry, but removing the selected proof step failed!"` / `"Can't delete the given theory path!"`. This reconciles the §13/§14 version model — del/path is a proof op, **not** an in-place structural edit like POST `edit/delete`.
